response.justification: The candidate brings 17+ years of broad full-stack engineering experience and genuine entrepreneurial initiative through founding an open-source GIS platform and active community teaching, but falls significantly short of the role's core requirements. AI involvement is limited to a single recent role (April 2025 onward) focused on MCP tooling, with no track record of shipping AI-powered products or platforms at scale. There is no evidence of director-level scope: no strategic customer advisory work, no cross-organizational technical leadership, and no demonstrated influence on product or cloud strategy beyond individual contributor delivery. The role demands recognized AI expertise and the ability to set technical direction for enterprise customers; the resume does not support that profile.
strengths: 17+ years of continuous software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — comfortably clears the experience floor
Founded and maintains GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform running on Kubernetes, demonstrating entrepreneurial drive and end-to-end product ownership
Active community contributor through conference talks, full-day workshops, and Dojo sessions, showing an ability to inspire and teach peers
concerns: AI expertise is narrow and very recent — MCP tooling at current employer since April 2025 is the entirety of demonstrated AI work; no shipped AI products or platforms at scale
No evidence of director-level impact: no strategic customer advisory, no cross-org technical leadership, and no influence on cloud or AI product roadmaps
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and the practical AI depth does not compensate; cloud experience is limited to AWS CDK usage and Kubernetes self-hosting rather than platform-level expertise
